title: mentha × villosa var. genevensis
intro: Mentha × villosa var. genevensis, also known as Geneva mint or peppermint, is a hybrid of the peppermint species (Mentha × piperita) and a wild mint variety. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural digestive aid, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Mentha × villosa var. genevensis include alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, relieving headaches and nausea, and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared as teas, infusions, or capsules, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy and as a flavoring agent in food and beverages.
